columns.description: America/New_York date derived from the immutable Bronze manifest completed_at timestamp, never from a latest-only request date label.
columns.name: capture_date

columns.description: Provider asset class preserved verbatim; the reviewed request is expected to contain stocks.
columns.name: asset_class

columns.description: Provider condition namespace copied verbatim from type; it is part of the identity because numeric IDs overlap across namespaces.
columns.name: condition_type

columns.description: Positive provider condition identifier copied verbatim from id and interpreted only within condition_type.
columns.name: condition_id

columns.description: Provider legacy flag normalized to false only when the field is absent; current and legacy definitions remain distinct rows.
columns.name: is_legacy

columns.description: One verbatim provider label expanded from data_types without mapping or eligibility inference.
columns.name: data_type

columns.description: True only when the provider source object explicitly contains legacy, preserving absent versus explicit false.
columns.name: legacy_source_present

columns.description: Zero-based ordinal of data_type in the original provider data_types array.
columns.name: source_data_type_ordinal

columns.description: Fixed current_reference_snapshot marker prohibiting interpretation as a historical condition-definition table.
columns.name: snapshot_scope

columns.description: Immutable Bronze manifest completed_at timestamp used as the conservative source observation time.
columns.name: source_capture_at_utc

columns.description: First XNYS session whose open is strictly after source_capture_at_utc.
columns.name: available_session

columns.description: XNYS open timestamp for available_session; this snapshot cannot be used before this instant.
columns.name: available_at_utc

columns.description: Fixed first_xnys_open_after_source_capture_v1 point-in-time availability rule.
columns.name: availability_rule

columns.description: Deterministic SHA-256 over dataset, source request, artifact digest, page sequence, row ordinal, and source row hash.
columns.name: source_record_id

columns.description: Canonical local Bronze request ID from the authoritative manifest.
columns.name: source_request_id

columns.description: Server-assigned request ID from the Massive response envelope.
columns.name: source_provider_request_id

columns.description: SHA-256 of the stored gzip Bronze page containing the source row.
columns.name: source_artifact_sha256

columns.description: Zero-based sequence of the source page in the Bronze manifest.
columns.name: source_page_sequence

columns.description: Zero-based ordinal of the source result within its page.
columns.name: source_row_ordinal

columns.description: SHA-256 of the canonical JSON representation of the unmodified provider result object.
columns.name: source_row_hash

description: Normalized source-order membership between version-preserved condition definitions and Massive data types, with conservative point-in-time availability and row-level Bronze lineage.
grain: One provider data-type membership per America/New_York capture date, asset_class, condition_type, condition_id, is_legacy, and data_type.

qa_rules.description: Every output Parquet field, order, Arrow type, and nullability must equal this contract.
No authoritative manifest or manifest-bound artifact may fail status, bytes, checksum, or declared-row validation.
Every source page must have an OK envelope, results array, provider request ID, and reconciled count.
Each capture date must be backed by exactly one authoritative current-only source request in a build.
Input source rows must reconcile exactly to accepted rows, exact duplicate excess, and quarantined rows.
Every row must have valid identity fields required by this table without silent string or integer coercion.
When present, legacy must be a native JSON boolean; absence alone is normalized to false and recorded separately.
data_types must be a nonempty source-order array of unique, nonblank strings.
No frozen natural key may map to differing canonical source rows; legacy is part of the key.
The output must contain no duplicate excess for the frozen primary key.
Every output row must reproduce its request, page, ordinal, artifact, row, and record digests.
Capture and availability fields must follow the frozen calendar and first-open-after-capture rule.
Every output row must carry the exact current_reference_snapshot scope marker.
Every reviewed row must retain the stocks asset class requested from the provider.
Previously unseen condition namespaces are retained verbatim but require explicit schema review.
Previously unseen data-type labels are retained verbatim but require explicit schema review.
Rows with provider fields outside the reviewed mapping are retained with lineage and surfaced for review.
Canonical duplicate source rows are deterministically deduplicated and remain visible in the row funnel.
Every distinct current or legacy natural-key version in accepted source rows must remain represented in the output.
Bridge rows must equal the sum of data_types lengths across accepted source rows.
Every bridge ordinal must point back to the exact source-order data_types value.
Every bridge row must resolve to a condition_code_dim parent key produced by the same transform.
Every condition_code_dim parent produced by the same transform must have at least one bridge membership.
